headline_drivers: Fiscal Q2 2026 earnings beat and outlook raise: EPS $0.16 vs $0.09 consensus, revenue $659.4M vs $655.3M expected, and FY 2026 revenue guidance raised to around $2.7B (Vestis press release and earnings call, 2026-05-12)
Multiple analyst target hikes following the print: Robert W. Baird lifting target from $10 to $14 (neutral) and Stifel Nicolaus raising from $8.50 to $11 (MarketBeat summary, 2026-05-13)

Momentum/technical re-rating on high volume: stock up ~29% on the day with ~13x average volume, appearing on top-1d and volume-spike screens after a >50% 60‑day move from a prior depressed base (StockInvest/MarketBeat price and volume data, mid‑May 2026)
summary: The move in Vestis is being driven primarily by a clean beat-and-raise quarter plus a valuation re‑rating from the sell side. On May 12 the company reported Q2 2026 EPS of $0.16 vs $0.09 expected and slightly better‑than‑forecast revenue of $659.4M, while raising its full‑year 2026 revenue outlook to roughly $2.7B, which helped validate a turnaround narrative after prior weakness. That print triggered a wave of analyst price-target increases (e.g., Baird to $14, Stifel to $11), signaling that the Street is reassessing fair value higher from what had been a discounted multiple. The 30%+ one‑day jump on ~13x normal volume suggests the market is now repricing the stock on this improved earnings trajectory and sentiment shift, with technical momentum and short-term traders amplifying the move rather than any separate big-ticket customer, M&A, or regulatory catalyst.
